steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two sculptures. The correct choice must explicitly contrast them — not describe what they share.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Simon Hartley and Mara Lin are artists who have used neon in their sculptures.: States a similarity (both used neon). ✗
  • Choice B — Unlike Hartley's sculpture, which spells out a phrase, Lin's is an abstract squiggle shape.: Directly contrasts the two works by opposing “spells out a phrase” against “abstract squiggle shape.” ✓
  • Choice C — Both Hartley's and Lin's sculptures are crafted from neon lighting.: States a similarity (both use neon). ✗
  • Choice D — *Never Sleep*, like *Verdurous No. 2*, is a sculpture.: States a similarity (both are sculptures). ✗
That matches Choice B — Unlike Hartley's sculpture, which spells out a phrase, Lin's is an abstract squiggle shape..
